era: 1992-1998. Grunge. E=7 -- heavy, mid-tempo, Sub Pop secondary roster.
associated_musicians: Phil Ek (producer: Built to Spill, Fleet Foxes, Band of Horses, The Shins)

theory_nodes.concept: Phil Ek career arc: Pond drummer (1992-1995) to Pacific Northwest's most important producer. Built to Spill, Fleet Foxes, Band of Horses. Founding-moment grunge band as producer training ground.
theory_nodes.description: Pond produces the PNW's most influential indie producer of the 2000s.

theory_nodes.concept: Sub Pop secondary roster: grunge as reproducible form. Pond documents what the genre sounded like as a community of bands, not just as a handful of exceptional acts. E=7 = competent form execution.
theory_nodes.description: Grunge as transferable genre, documented by secondary roster.

theory_nodes.concept: Fremont second consecutive entry (321: Gas Huffer/AmRep, 322: Pond/Sub Pop). Two Fremont labels in two degrees. Neighborhood's range documented.
theory_nodes.description: Fremont chapter complete: AmRep + Sub Pop in consecutive degrees.

cross_references.relationship: Fremont consecutive. AmRep vs. Sub Pop. Noise-rock humor vs. conventional craft. E=7 holds.
E rises to 8. E=7 plateau broken. Post-summit arc reaches back to 1982 proto-grunge.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 S36 vs. E=8 SPS. Geophysics exceeds the working grunge band by one unit. The mountain is not competing; it simply exceeds.

milestones: PHIL EK FIRST APPEARANCE IN ENGINE
